001package guiEmailLogin;
002
003import javafx.stage.Stage;
004import applicationMain.ValidationUtil;
005import database.Database;
006import entityClasses.User;
007import guiUserLogin.ViewUserLogin;
008import guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ViewMultipleRoleDispatch;
009
010/*******
011 * <p> Title: ControllerEmailLogin Class. </p>
012 * 
013 * <p> Description: The Controller for the Email Login page.</p>
014 * 
015 * <p> Copyright: Modified 2026 </p>
016 * 
017 * @version 1.00                2026-02-09 Initial version
018 *  
019 */
020
021public class ControllerEmailLogin {
022        
023        // Reference for the in-memory database
024        private static Database theDatabase = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.database;
025        
026        /**********
027         * <p> Method: doEmailLogin(Stage theStage) </p>
028         * 
029         * <p> Description: This method validates the email and password, and if valid, logs the user in.</p>
030         * 
031         * @param theStage specifies the JavaFX Stage
032         * 
033         */
034        public static void doEmailLogin(Stage theStage) {
035                
036                // Get the entered values
037                String email = ViewEmailLogin.text_Email.getText().trim();
038                String password = ViewEmailLogin.text_Password.getText();
039                
040                // Validate email address
041                String emailError = ValidationUtil.validateEmail(email);
042                if (emailError != null) {
043                        ViewEmailLogin.alertLoginError.setContentText(emailError);
044                        ViewEmailLogin.alertLoginError.showAndWait();
045                        return;
046                }
047                
048                // Validate password
049                String passwordError = ValidationUtil.validatePassword(password);
050                if (passwordError != null) {
051                        ViewEmailLogin.alertLoginError.setContentText(passwordError);
052                        ViewEmailLogin.alertLoginError.showAndWait();
053                        return;
054                }
055                
056                // Look up user by email address
057                // First, we need to get the username associated with this email
058                String username = theDatabase.getUsernameByEmail(email);
059                
060                if (username == null || username.isEmpty()) {
061                        ViewEmailLogin.alertLoginError.setContentText("Invalid email or password.");
062                        ViewEmailLogin.alertLoginError.showAndWait();
063                        return;
064                }
065                
066                // Fetch the user account details
067                if (!theDatabase.getUserAccountDetails(username)) {
068                        ViewEmailLogin.alertLoginError.setContentText("Invalid email or password.");
069                        ViewEmailLogin.alertLoginError.showAndWait();
070                        return;
071                }
072                
073                // Check to see that the login password matches the account password
074                String actualPassword = theDatabase.getCurrentPassword();
075                
076                if (!password.equals(actualPassword)) {
077                        ViewEmailLogin.alertLoginError.setContentText("Invalid email or password.");
078                        ViewEmailLogin.alertLoginError.showAndWait();
079                        return;
080                }
081                
082                // Establish this user's details
083                User user = new User(username, password, theDatabase.getCurrentFirstName(), 
084                                theDatabase.getCurrentMiddleName(), theDatabase.getCurrentLastName(), 
085                                theDatabase.getCurrentPreferredFirstName(), theDatabase.getCurrentEmailAddress(), 
086                                theDatabase.getCurrentAdminRole(), 
087                                theDatabase.getCurrentStudentRole(), theDatabase.getCurrentStaffRole());
088                
089                // See which home page dispatch to use
090                int numberOfRoles = theDatabase.getNumberOfRoles(user);
091                
092                if (numberOfRoles == 1) {
093                        // Single Account Home Page - The user has no choice here
094                        
095                        // Admin role
096                        if (user.getAdminRole()) {
097                                theDatabase.loginAdmin(user);
098                                guiAdminHome.ViewAdminHome.displayAdminHome(theStage, user);
099                        } else if (user.getStudentRole()) {
100                                theDatabase.loginStudent(user);
101                                guiStudentHome.ViewStudentHome.displayStudentHome(theStage, user);
102                        } else if (user.getStaffRole()) {
103                                theDatabase.loginStaff(user);
104                                guiStaffHome.ViewStaffHome.displayStaffHome(theStage, user);
105                        } else {
106                                System.out.println("***** EmailLogin has an invalid role");
107                        }
108                } else if (numberOfRoles > 1) {
109                        // Multiple Account Home Page - The user chooses which role to play
110                        ViewMultipleRoleDispatch.displayMultipleRoleDispatch(theStage, user);
111                }
112        }
113        
114        /**********
115         * <p> Method: performCancel(Stage theStage) </p>
116         * 
117         * <p> Description: This method returns to the login page.</p>
118         * 
119         * @param theStage specifies the JavaFX Stage
120         * 
121         */
122        public static void performCancel(Stage theStage) {
123                ViewUserLogin.displayUserLogin(theStage);
124        }
125}
